Title: Setosphaeria turcica ATR turns off appressorium-mediated maize infection and triggers melanin-involved self-protection in response to genotoxic stress

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Robustly induced by hydroxyurea (HU)-mediated genotoxic stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Robustly induced by HU; induction reduced/restored in StATR RNAi mutant under HU
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DNA genotoxic stress; hydroxyurea (HU) treatment; ATR inhibition/knockdown context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Self-protection against genotoxic stress; contributes to appressorial turgor and pathogenicity under normal infection conditions
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: DHN-melanin branch; de novo synthesis of DHN precursor
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes de novo polyketide synthesis leading to formation of the phenolic precursor 1,8-dihydroxynaphthalene (DHN) for melanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: DHN pathway polyketide intermediate(s) leading to 1,8-dihydroxynaphthalene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical enzyme; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StPKS transcript was robustly induced (~4-fold) by HU-induced genotoxic stress in the wild type. 2. This HU-induced up-regulation was reduced/restored in the StATR RNAi mutant, identifying StPKS as an ATR-dependent checkpoint target. 3. StPKS promoter contains four CGCG motifs and is directly targeted by StMbp1, linking checkpoint signaling to melanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under HU; promoter motif analysis; electrophoretic mobility shift assay; yeast one-hybrid; StATR RNAi comparison
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target in combination strategies that disrupt stress-induced protective melanin biosynthesis; may be more selective than targeting conserved ATR directly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing PKS-mediated melanin may weaken fungal stress protection and pathogenicity; no direct fitness cost quantified for StPKS manipulation in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a novel ATR-mediated S-phase checkpoint target; promoter binding by StMbp1 is supported, unlike StLac2 which lacks Mbp1 motif evidence.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Robustly induced by HU-mediated genotoxic stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by HU in wild type; regulation by ATR suggested by expression changes in StATR RNAi background
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Self-protection against genotoxic stress; contributes to appressorial melanization and pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Polymerization/final assembly branch of DHN-melanin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively polymerizes DHN-derived monomers during melanin formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential gene for polymerizing DHN monomers; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes stress-induced and pathogenicity-associated DHN-melanin formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StLac2 transcript was induced by approximately 4-fold by HU in the wild type. 2. StLac2 was identified as a critical melanin-pathway gene associated with ATR-mediated stress response. 3. Unlike StPKS, no Mbp1-binding motif was found in its promoter, and direct ATR-MBF regulation was not demonstrated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under HU; pathway assignment from prior functional work discussed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream antifungal target to block protective melanin accumulation and infection-related melanization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct manipulation in this paper; prior work cited indicates importance for pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states they did not find evidence of direct ATR-dependent regulation via Mbp1; promoter lacks CGCG motif for Mbp1 binding, so regulation mechanism remains unresolved.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed through RNAi knockdown; transcript reduced by 35% in RNAi#21 and 85% in RNAi#40
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DNA genotoxic stress induced by hydroxyurea (HU), methyl methane-sulfonate (MMS), camptothecin (CPT), and UV; caffeine used as ATR/checkpoint inhibitor
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protects cells against genotoxic stress; melanin also supports appressorial turgor during infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PI3K-like serine/threonine protein kinase; ATR/Rad3 family
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: DNA replication stress; DNA damage; genotoxic reagents (HU, MMS, CPT) and UV
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: StPKS; StLac2; StRNR1; DNA repair-related genes; appressorium initiation program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates stress-response transcription including StRNR1 and promotes induction of StPKS and StLac2 under HU; represses appressorium initiation under genotoxic stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Checkpoint kinase signaling; transcriptional activation of stress-response genes; indirect suppression of developmental infection program
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Melanin biosynthesis and stress-adaptive metabolic response; infection-related developmental suppression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in conserved ATR-Cds1-Nrm1-Mbp1 checkpoint cascade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central checkpoint effector required for stress-induced melanin accumulation and for turning off appressorium-mediated infection under genotoxic stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melanin biosynthesis in response to genotoxic stress and suppresses appressorium formation/infection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Genotoxic stress activated an ATR-dependent S-phase checkpoint in S. turcica. 2. Pharmacological inhibition or RNAi knockdown of StATR restored appressorium formation in the presence of HU, showing StATR is required for stress-induced repression of infection. 3. StATR promoted melanin accumulation and induction of melanin genes under stress, supporting a self-protection function.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; pharmacological inhibition with caffeine; qRT-PCR; appressorium assays; maize infection assay; melanin quantification; growth sensitivity assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathway for antifungal intervention, but ATR itself may be a poor direct target because of strong conservation; downstream branch-specific targets may be preferable
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: StATR knockdown increased susceptibility to HU, restored appressorium initiation under stress, but appressoria were defective, less melanized, and impaired in penetration/infection
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study emphasizes source and applied species are the same fungus, while maize is only the host used in infection assays; StATR is not a plant nutrient gene but regulates fungal melanin metabolism under stress.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: StPKS promoter (native, inducible/stress responsive target promoter studied)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: HU-induced genotoxic stress in ATR checkpoint context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Self-protection against genotoxic stress; supports infection-related melanization
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: StPKS promoter containing four CGCG motifs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/positively regulates StPKS transcription under checkpoint signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional regulation by promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Component of MBF; interacts with StNrm1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct link between ATR checkpoint signaling and melanin biosynthetic gene activation through StPKS promoter targeting
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes StPKS expression and thereby supports stress-induced melanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The StPKS promoter contains four Mbp1-binding CGCG motifs. 2. StMbp1 directly bound the StPKS promoter in EMSA and yeast one-hybrid assays. 3. StMbp1 interacted with StNrm1, supporting a conserved ATR-Cds1-Nrm1-Mbp1 transcriptional cascade controlling melanin induction.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif search; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; GST pull-down/mass spectrometry; yeast two-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential branch-specific antifungal target to disrupt protective melanin induction more selectively than inhibiting ATR
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct mutant phenotype tested in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named as StMbp1/StMBP1 in text; direct regulation supported only for StPKS, not StLac2.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely modulates MBF-dependent transcription in checkpoint signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Stress-induced melanin biosynthesis via MBF pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Bridges checkpoint kinase signaling to MBF-dependent melanin gene regulation in the proposed model
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ATR-Cds1-Mbp1 signaling leading to StPKS induction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StNrm1 was captured as an StMbp1-associated protein in GST pull-down and confirmed by yeast two-hybrid. 2. StNrm1 also interacted with StCds1. 3. These data support a conserved checkpoint signaling cascade controlling melanin induction through MBF.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GST pull-down with mass spectrometry; yeast two-hybrid; in vitro protein-binding assay with StNrm1ΔC
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential indirect target within fungal-specific stress-adaptation branch, though no direct phenotype validation was provided
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct mutant analysis in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally characterized in Results as a key mediator in the regulatory cascade to melanin biosynthesis, although direct metabolite phenotype was not individually tested.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Protein kinase; downstream ATR checkpoint kinase
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: StATR-mediated S-phase checkpoint activation by genotoxic stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly promotes MBF/StMbp1-dependent StPKS expression through Nrm1 pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Checkpoint kinase signaling via protein interaction cascade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as the downstream checkpoint kinase linking ATR to the Nrm1-Mbp1 branch controlling melanin-gene induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ATR-mediated signaling to induce melanin biosynthetic regulation under stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StCds1 physically interacted with StNrm1 in yeast two-hybrid and in vitro assays. 2. This places StCds1 in the conserved ATR signaling branch upstream of MBF-dependent StPKS regulation. 3. Its role in melanin induction is supported mechanistically but not by direct mutant metabolite assays in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; in vitro protein-binding assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathway component for antifungal targeting, but likely conserved and thus less selective than downstream components
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct phenotype validation in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core mechanistic regulator experimentally analyzed in Results; direct causal metabolite perturbation was inferred through pathway placement rather than individual genetic manipulation here.
